for_verification_agent.acceptance_criteria: Three services (engine, api, agents) each build a working container image via Dockerfile; container starts and responds to a basic health check.
for_verification_agent.test_spec: docker build succeeds for each Dockerfile; docker run + basic health-check curl/script confirms service starts.

modified_files.change: added HealthHandler + GET /health registration in RegisterRoutes
modified_files.line_ranges: new HealthHandler function immediately before RegisterRoutes (near end of file, ~line 92-105); RegisterRoutes gained one new mux.HandleFunc line

build_contexts.engine: repo root; docker build -f deploy/engine.Dockerfile -t hivemind-engine .
build_contexts.api: repo root (needs engine/rpc/gen cross-module import + go.work/go.work.sum); docker build -f deploy/api.Dockerfile -t hivemind-api .
build_contexts.agents: repo root; docker build -f deploy/agents.Dockerfile -t hivemind-agents .

health_check_mechanisms.engine: gRPC-only, no HTTP -- Docker HEALTHCHECK is a dependency-free `nc -z 127.0.0.1 50051` TCP-connect check (busybox nc, no new deps)
health_check_mechanisms.api: new GET /health HTTP route (api/routes/query.go's HealthHandler) returning 200 'ok'; Docker HEALTHCHECK uses busybox `wget`
health_check_mechanisms.agents: gRPC-only (agents/query/server.py), no HTTP -- Docker HEALTHCHECK is a dependency-free python3 socket.create_connection TCP-connect check; grpcio-health-checking was deliberately NOT added as a new dependency (not in uv.lock, cannot resolve a new PyPI lock entry without a real network `uv lock` run in this environment)

for_verification_agent.important_caveat_for_agents_service: agents/query/server.py's serve() constructs the LLM client (llm.factory.create_llm_client()) BEFORE binding the gRPC port. A bare `docker run` of deploy/agents.Dockerfile with NO env vars set will exit(1) immediately with llm.factory.LLMFactoryError ('No LLM provider configured') and NEVER pass a health check. It only starts listening (and then passes the health check) once LLM_PROVIDER is set to one of ollama/openrouter/gemini -- LLM_PROVIDER=ollama was used during self-consistency because OllamaClient's constructor does not require an API key (only openrouter/gemini would additionally need a key env var). This was NOT hidden -- see self-consistency.json's run_unconfigured vs run_configured results. Verification agent should decide whether this behavior (health check requires LLM_PROVIDER to be set) is acceptable for this subtask's scope or whether it should be flagged as a finding (e.g. suggesting docker-compose.yml, subtask 6.2.2, is the natural place to set LLM_PROVIDER as a default env var for local/demo runs).

self_consistency_summary.all_three_builds: SUCCESS (real `docker build`, Docker Desktop daemon started and used in this environment)
self_consistency_summary.all_three_health_checks_passed: engine: PASS; api: PASS; agents: PASS ONLY with -e LLM_PROVIDER=ollama (documented above, not hidden)

not_in_scope_this_subtask: Real deploy target host (subtask 6.2.3, blocked on OQ-3)
grpc_health_v1 service registration in engine/rpc or agents/query (documented design decision, not silently skipped -- see architecture-discovery.md)
